channel.title: Epic Fury Live — US-Iran War Tracker
channel.description: Live tracking of the US-Israel-Iran conflict. Real-time updates, verified casualties, evidence, and analysis.

item.title: Trump says he is keeping Strait of Hormuz closed, not Iran
item.description: Donald Trump said he is the one keeping the Strait of Hormuz closed, not Iran.

item.title: Iran dismisses Trump’s claim of leadership rift, says nation is ‘one soul’
item.description: Several Iranian officials share same message, emphasising what they call the 'iron unity of the nation and government'.

item.title: How Trump’s Iran war is driving military dissent
item.description: From protests to quiet resistance, dissent is rising inside the United States military over the US-Israel war on Iran.

item.title: Mixed views in Lebanon ahead of controversial talks with Israel
item.description: Lebanon remains divided over the best way to end the conflict with Israel, with some backing Hezbollah's armed response.

item.title: Italy slams Trump envoy proposal of taking Iran’s place in World Cup 2026
item.description: Italy officials slam suggestion that national football team should replace Iran at World Cup; fans also underwhelmed.

item.title: US-Israeli war on Iran will push 30 million back into poverty, UN warns
item.description: Disruption to fuel and fertiliser supplies due to the Strait of Hormuz closure will hit crop yields, UNDP chief warns.

item.title: Journalist recalls Israeli strike killing Amal Khalil in southern Lebanon
item.description: Journalist Zainab Faraj recalled the final moments of fellow journalist Amal Khalil.

item.title: Lebanon Latest: Israel attacks kill journalist and target first responders
item.description: A fragile ceasefire holds as Israeli strikes kill a journalist and target first responders in Lebanon.

item.title: In Tehran, money is short and a return to war looms over daily life
item.description: The BBC's Lyse Doucet reports from Iran, where the buzz of busy shopping streets masks deep uncertainty over the country's future.

item.title: More than 500 people killed in Tanzania election violence, inquiry finds
item.description: Opposition parties accused security forces but the official report does not say who was responsible.
